name: Spaghetti with Clam Sauce
recipeIngredient: in place of butter and olive oil: no cholesterol)
1 1/2 cups clams, fresh or canned (I like to use 2 cans of Chicken of the Sea baby clams)
1 cup clam juice, fresh or bottled
1/2 cup freshly grated low-fat Parmesan cheese (optional, but delicious)
recipeInstructions:
1. Grate the Parmesan cheese and set aside.
2. Mince the parsley and set aside.
3. Mince the garlic.
4. Melt the butter or margarine in a saucepan over medium heat.
5. Add olive oil, pepper, oregano, basil, minced garlic, clams, and clam juice to the melted butter.
6. Simmer for 30 minutes until the clams are cooked through.
7. Taste and adjust salt as needed.
8. Warm a large bowl in the microwave or on the stovetop.
9. Rinse and drain the spaghetti, then add it to the bowl with the clam sauce.
10. Toss with parsley and serve immediately.
11. Optional: Sprinkle shredded Parmesan cheese over the pasta before serving. Leftovers keep well (refrigerated) and heat up nicely in a microwave.
